properties.remark: VINTON POLICE DEPT REPORTS A TRAILER FLIPPED OVER IN VINTON. THEY ALSO REPORTED POLICE VEHICLES MOVED BY STRONG WINDS.
A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS CAUSED SOME TREES TO FALL DOWN AND SEVERAL HOMES OR SHED RECEIVED ROOF DAMAGE.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH 110 MPH WINDS FLIPPED OVER A SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME, DESTROYING IT ON GRAND BOIS ROAD NEAR PARKS. FORTUNATELY NO ONE WAS HOME. SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN AND SEVERAL OTHER HOMES AND OUT BUILDINGS RECEIVED ROOF DAMAGE ALONG NURSERY HIGHWAY.
KARA MEASURED A 60KT PEAK WIND GUST.
WATER OVER THE ROAD AT TANK FARM RD AND TANGUERAY RD
AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE TOWN OFWASHINGTON, RIPPING SEVERAL HOMES ROOFS AND SNAPPING THE TRUNKS OF SEVERAL TREES
CAPL1 RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS.
SEVERAL CARS FLOODED ON KIRKMAN STREET NEAR DOWNTOWN. WATER ALSO REPORTED TO BE BUMPER DEEP TO SUVS ON BROAD STREET NEAR DOWNTOWN.
PUBLIC REPORTS A ROOF BLOWN OFF A HOME ON CHURCH ST IN WASHINGTON.
TREES REPORTED DOWN ON POWER LINES JUST SOUTH OF BOYKIN SPRINGS RECREATIONAL AREA.
TREES REPORTED DOWN ON POWER LINES JUST SOUTH OF BOYKIN SPRINGS RECREATIONAL AREA.

TREES DOWN ON POWER LINES ABOUT 10 MILES SOUTH-SOUTHWEST OF ANACOCO, LOUISIANA.
AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG LAMOURIE RD,REMOVING THE ROOF OF A SMALL BARN AND FLIPPING OVER AN UNTIED SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AS WELL.
MINOR WIND RELATED DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS AND SIGNS ON THE SOUTHSIDE OF MARKSVILLE. DAMAGE WAS SEEN AT THE SONIC DRIVE-IN AS WELL AS THE AVOYELLES PARISH 911 OFFICE. THIS WAS POSSIBLY DUE TO A TORNADO.
MINOR WIND RELATED DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS AND SIGNS ON THE SOUTHSIDE OF MARKSVILLE. DAMAGE WAS SEEN AT THE SONIC DRIVE-IN AS WELL AS THE AVOYELLES PARISH 911 OFFICE. THIS WAS NOT A TORNADO.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE CHARTER SCHOOL,DAMAGING SOME OF THE EXTERIOR WALLS. SEVERAL ROOFS OF OUTSIDE BALLFIELDS WERE DESTROYED. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED NEAR OLD RIVER ROAD EAST OF THE SCHOOL.
MINOR WIND RELATED DAMAGE AT THE AVOYELLES PUBLIC CHARTER SCHOOL LOCATED NEAR MANSURA. THIS WAS POSSIBLY DUE TO A TORNADO.
AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HWY 451 AND PETELABORDE RD. ONE HOME LOST ITS ROOF. SEVERAL BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS LOST ROOFS. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.
ROOF OFF OF A BUILDING ON PETE LABORDE RD NEAR BORDELONVILLE. THIS WAS POSSIBLY DUE TO A TORNADO.

A TEAM DOING RESEARCH WITH NSSL FOUND DAMAGE WITH THIS TORNADO.MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS TREE DAMAGE WEST OF HWY 71 AND CROSSINGHWY 71. IT DID DESTROY A BARN BEFORE THE DAMAGE PATH WAS LOST NEAR HWY 361.
TDS DECTED FROM KPOE LAST FOR AT LEAST 12 MINUTES.

*** 1 FATAL, 7 INJ *** A STRONG TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON BOLDEN ROAD, DESTROYING SEVERAL HOMES. SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME THAT WAS TIED DOWN ROLLED NUMEROUS TIMES OVER 320 YARDS. ONE MAN DIED IN THIS HOME. DOUBLE WIDE MOBILE HOME NEXT DOOR ROLLED OVER 100 YARDS. TWO PEOPLE WERE SERIOUSLY INJURED. THIRD HOME ON PILINGS SLID OVER 50 YARDS, WITH 5 PEOPLE INSIDE, WHO RECEIVED CUTS AND BRUISES. IN TOTAL, 15 HOMES DAMAGED/DESTROYED AS WELL AS TREES AND POWER LINES SNAPPED AND BARNS/OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED. PEAK WIND 140 MPH.
